rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical sulfonylation/cyclization of alkenes with sodium sulfinates proceeding via sulfonyl radical addition under undivided-cell conditions.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinate (ArSO2Na) to a sulfonyl radical (ArSO2•), which adds to the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: tosyl radical (ArSO2•) from anodic oxidation of sodium p-toluenesulfinate
benzylic/cyclopropylcarbinyl C-radical from sulfonyl radical addition to 1-cyclopropyl-1-phenylethylene
vinyl sulfone product 9 (sulfonyl-trapped alkene, no ring-opening observed)

electron_flow: Sulfinate is oxidized at the anode by SET to generate a sulfonyl radical that adds to the alkene; the resulting carbon radical is further oxidized/deprotonated to the vinyl sulfone, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: 1-cyclopropyl-1-phenylethylene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction with sodium p-toluenesulfinate under electrolysis (GF anode, Pt cathode, Et4NClO4, HFIP/H2O 20:1, 4 mA, 8 h) gave the vinyl sulfone 9 without cyclopropane ring-opening rearrangement, indicating fast trapping of the sulfonyl-adduct radical or a non-radical pathway at that stage.

notes: Page also contains characterization data for Suzuki cross-coupling product 6 (Pd(OAc)2/PPh3/K2CO3, i-PrOH, 90 °C) of bromo-thiazonine 4l with (3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)boronic acid — this is product diversification, not mechanistic. Only the radical trapping/clock experiment at the bottom is mechanism-relevant.
